title: Direct race-lap ranking model instead of post-hoc query rank blending
summary: Your code already exploits within-`Year/Race/LapNumber` structure after training, but public ranking literature and CatBoost ranking objectives suggest learning that structure directly.
rationale: Your code already exploits within-`Year/Race/LapNumber` structure after training, but public ranking literature and CatBoost ranking objectives suggest learning that structure directly. In each lap-group, the model should learn which drivers are most likely to pit next, not just independent probabilities.
implementation_hint: Build a separate ranker with group/query id = `Year_Race_LapNumber`; start with CatBoost `QuerySoftMax` or `YetiRankPairwise`, and optionally a LambdaMART baseline. Evaluate both query metrics and downstream ROC AUC from the ranker’s scores; then blend ranker OOF with the current classifier OOF.
expected_effect: A genuinely different expert that can improve relative ordering inside each lap, where many of the hard mistakes likely live.
risk: Most lap-groups have few positives, sometimes zero; ranking objectives can become unstable unless you filter/weight queries carefully and keep a classifier expert in the ensemble.
